design_note.primitive: The agenda-setting unit in oncology is the asset-modality-catalyst, NOT the company. Entities exist here only as subscription addresses — the IR pages, SEC filers and regulator feeds where catalysts surface. The catalyst_queue is the product; this roster is plumbing.
design_note.why_not_companies: Readouts reprice the sector, companies react. daraxonrasib survives Revolution Medicines being acquired; PD-1xVEGF spans nine companies and no single ticker covers it.
design_note.sizing: ~24 standing entities. Below ~20 loses a tier; above ~30 adds entities that have never once moved the market.

self_evolution.rule: Radar entities may be promoted into `entities` by the loop without human approval; every promotion appends to `drift_log` with a reason. Demotion after `quiet_cycles_before_review` cycles of silence is proposed, not automatic, for anything in tier acquirer/regulator.

tiers.acquirer: Can reprice a modality by writing a cheque.
tiers.china_supply: Where the clearing price of an asset class is discovered.
tiers.regulator: Changes universal behaviour by fiat — highest signal-per-item.
tiers.frontier_asset: Tracked as an asset, not a ticker, because the tickers keep disappearing.

entities.name: Merck & Co.
entities.why_tracked: The forced buyer: >$25B/yr Keytruda revenue faces the 2028 LOE cliff. Every Merck move is clock-driven and sets the sector's bid.

entities.name: Eli Lilly
entities.why_tracked: Largest acquirer in biotech by capital deployed in H1 2026 — mostly OUTSIDE oncology. Sets the clearing price everyone else pays without setting the oncology agenda.

entities.name: AstraZeneca
entities.why_tracked: Daiichi's 50/50 partner across HER2/TROP2/B7-H3 — the commercial half of the ADC agenda.

entities.name: Pfizer
entities.why_tracked: Post-Seagen ADC estate plus a $1.25B-upfront 3SBio PD-1xVEGF bet; among the most aggressive Keytruda-successor bidders.

entities.name: GSK
entities.why_tracked: Behaviour change in progress under new leadership: $10.6B Nuvalent, $12.5B Hengrui alliance. A follower turning buyer is itself a signal.

entities.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
entities.why_tracked: Deepest China exposure of any US pharma ($15.2B Hengrui alliance, up-to-$11B BioNTech pumitamig).

entities.name: Jiangsu Hengrui Pharma
entities.why_tracked: The volume price-setter. Its mega-alliances define what a China portfolio costs.

entities.name: Akeso Biopharma
entities.why_tracked: The only Chinese biotech to trigger a Western pile-on and then deliver OS. Tetrabody platform + ivonescimab.

entities.name: Kelun-Biotech
entities.why_tracked: sac-TMT is Merck's TROP2 anchor and China-approved — the live test of whether a China ADC wins globally.

entities.name: DualityBio
entities.why_tracked: ADC out-licensing bellwether; prices bispecific ADCs before the West does.

entities.name: 3SBio
entities.why_tracked: The $1.25B Pfizer upfront reset China upfronts to Western levels.

entities.name: Daiichi Sankyo
entities.why_tracked: DXd linker-payload is the closest thing to an oncology picks-and-shovels monopoly; sets payload and target norms everyone clones.

entities.name: Novartis
entities.why_tracked: Radioligand incumbent (Pluvicto/Lutathera) plus Novartis Venture Fund seeding — platform owner and capital signal in one.

entities.name: Roche / Genentech
entities.why_tracked: ON WATCH, not conviction. Historic agenda-setter now behaving as a follower. Tracked to detect IF it re-enters as a setter.
entities.review_rule: Drop after two quiet cycles unless it re-enters as a setter.

entities.name: FDA Oncology Center of Excellence / CDER
entities.why_tracked: Changes universal behaviour by fiat. Project Optimus moved dose-optimisation from <20% to a majority of early-phase protocols in three years; refused Summit's PFS-only filing and killed it. Highest signal-per-item on this list.

entities.name: FDA Commissioner's Office / CNPV
entities.why_tracked: Compressed review timelines, and openly contested by the CDER director — an institutional fracture with direct filing-strategy consequences.

entities.name: CMS / IRA negotiation
entities.why_tracked: The small-molecule-vs-biologic pill penalty shapes capital allocation across every oncology portfolio.

entities.name: daraxonrasib / RAS(ON) inhibitors
entities.why_tracked: HR 0.40 OS in metastatic pancreatic cancer. The most valuable single asset in oncology right now — and it survives whatever happens to its holder.

entities.name: ivonescimab / PD-1xVEGF
entities.why_tracked: The mechanism that triggered a >$10B pile-on before any OS win. HARMONi-6 delivered OS (HR 0.66) but is China-only — regional generalizability is THE contested question in IO.

entities.name: pumitamig & PF-08634404 (Western PD-1xVEGF)
entities.why_tracked: The two Western Phase 3s that will settle whether the >$10B pile-on was right.

entities.name: Radioligand / alpha-emitter platform
entities.why_tracked: Lilly's ~$100M anchor of the Aktis IPO marks radioligand as the next validated modality; Aktis is the pure-play read.

entities.name: ROTATING SLOT — bispecific ADCs / next-gen payloads
entities.why_tracked: Reserved slot. Promote a specific asset here on first human data; do not track the startups themselves pre-IND.

deliberately_off.why: EMA is a genuine follower. NMPA matters only as the generator of China readouts, which the china_supply tier already surfaces.

Capital without a readout has never moved another company's behaviour in oncology. This is the sharpest divergence from AI intuition: there, funding IS signal; here it is not. Revisit on primary-source-confirmed first-in-human dosing.

deliberately_off.what: VC firms as entities (Flagship, ARCH, RA Capital, OrbiMed, Forbion)
deliberately_off.why: Lagging indicators — they fund modalities the platform owners validated years earlier. Track aggregate flow, not individual funds. Novartis Venture Fund is captured inside the Novartis line.
Real companies, zero agenda-setting power pre-IND. They belong in the rotating slot's pipeline, not the standing roster.
Gone. Their assets live on inside the acquirer lines.
Big but not agenda-setting — the two are orthogonal. Akeso (mid-cap) moved four Big Pharmas; J&J (mega-cap) moved no one.
Could not be verified as a meaningful entity in any source. Do not seed.
